clean_body: Her staff called Capitol police on me and proceeded to ignore all of my questions before they were whisked away in a private SUV. | | | | | Dear Friend, While I was at the Capitol this week, I confronted Democrat Congresswoman Mary Peltola who recently "defeated" Sarah Palin to become the sole congresswoman from Alaska. Peltola used her Native American Heritage to win her race and claims that she is an advocate for Native Americans across the country . . . UNLESS that conflicts with her political agenda and protecting the D.C. establishment. The explosive story I asked her about would put her in the sights of the new RINO Speaker of the House, Corrupt Kevin McCarthy. McCarthy's family profited by accepting $7.6 million in federal contracts that were earmarked for Native American-owned, "disadvantaged" businesses. The company, Vortex Construction, is owned by William Wages, who happens to be Kevin McCarthy's brother-in-law. Wages claims to be Cherokee Native American, but according to tribal leader David Cornsilk, Wages isn't on the tribal rolls, and is instead part of an illegitimate group posing as Native American. In other words, McCarthy 's family conned their way to get their hands on lucrative federal contracts worth nearly $8 million! While Elizabeth Warren will forever be known as "Pocahontas", McCarthy has earned the nickname "Chief Sticky Fingers." But, when I asked Congresswoman Peltola if she's willing to investigate Kevin McCarthy and his family for this financial con job, she and her staff called Capitol police on me and proceeded to ignore all of my questions before they were whisked away in a private SUV. That's our corrupt, uni-party government folks! And I'll be working to expose their hypocrisy and fraud while I remain in D.C. But, funds are running tight, and Please Every dollar helps me continue my investigative journalism so I can continue exposing and fighting the D.C. establishment. Thanks again for all that you do. Respectfully, Laura Loomer (C) 2022 Illoominate Media 720 Lucerne Ave, #1465 LAKE WORTH, FL. 33460

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: I’m officially running for reelection
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wexton for Congress
body:
I wanted to share the official news with you personally:

**I’m running for reelection to continue fighting for you and your family in the U.S. Senate. In a minute, I’m going to ask you to become a Founding Donor to my reelection campaign. But first, please let me explain why your support _today_ is so important.**

The truth is that the road to keeping this seat blue isn’t going to be easy. During the 2022 midterms here in New York, Republicans won more races across the state than they have in two decades — enough to deliver the House majority back to the GOP. Now, they’re using that momentum to target this seat as a pickup opportunity in 2024 so that they can retake the Senate, too.

But I’ve never backed down from a challenge. My very first race for Congress was in a Republican district where people said that there were “more cows than Democrats.” I still ran, and we won. That’s because I had grassroots supporters like you by my side from the very beginning, making the early investments that are key to building a winning campaign.

So that’s what I’m asking of you today. With so much at stake, I’m counting on you to step up and become a Founding Donor today to help make sure I have the resources it will take to defend New York’s Senate seat and our Democratic majority.

**[Please, will you split $10 between my campaign and Jennifer Wexton’s to become a Founding Donor to my reelection campaign to defeat the GOP in New York and defend the Senate for Democrats?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57300868/387477089/-1578499571?refcode=EM_FR_230113KG_N_KS_FULL_9_NG_N_L1&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYWY2MmVmOC04YzkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=C7Rf9NvCW2d9EdDH1wN9rA1D07JKK0C6Qz7lZiiOxT4=)**

I’ve taken on some tough fights in the Senate: Securing health care for the 9/11 first responders. Repealing “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell.” Making sure Americans got paid family and medical leave during the pandemic.

But there are so many critical fights ahead — for voting rights, for reproductive freedom, for health care, for a permanent paid leave program. And I won’t let up until the fights are won. I promise you that.

**The GOP wants nothing more than to defeat me and halt progress on these critical issues, so I’m counting on your help to make sure we can take them head-on and win.[Please, will you split $10 between my campaign and Jennifer Wexton's to defend our Democratic Senate majority and make sure that I can keep fighting for you in the Senate?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57300869/387477090/-1578499571?refcode=EM_FR_230113KG_N_KS_FULL_9_NG_N_L2&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYWY2MmVmOC04YzkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=C7Rf9NvCW2d9EdDH1wN9rA1D07JKK0C6Qz7lZiiOxT4=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Kirsten Gillibrand and Jennifer Wexton:_ | | **[$10 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57300870/387477091/-1578499571?express_lane=true&refcode=EM_FR_230113KG_N_KS_FULL_9_NG_N_EB10&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYWY2MmVmOC04YzkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=C7Rf9NvCW2d9EdDH1wN9rA1D07JKK0C6Qz7lZiiOxT4=)**

body:

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. **** _**The 2023 Session: Culture Wars, Special Interests on the Docket - Not Fixing Indiana’s Most-Pressing Issues **_ **This week was the start of the 2023 Indiana General Assembly’s legislative session and to quote Leader Phil GiaQuinta: “Same stale ideas, different years.” The Republican supermajority presented their agenda for the legislative session. It included[opposition](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57301367/387478630/1793200108?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ODQ2NTJjYy04ZDkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hi3DBJB3TNK_GqUYvm6FuUbSpPXrgehVFuHYOWsRo_g=&emci=a339868d-8b93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=984652cc-8d93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) to Governor Holcomb’s calls to invest in the state’s lagging public health system.** **Fact is: Indiana’s future has dimmed under the GOP’s watch.** **Hoosiers have a[F rated quality-of-life](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57301368/387478631/-82093755?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ODQ2NTJjYy04ZDkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hi3DBJB3TNK_GqUYvm6FuUbSpPXrgehVFuHYOWsRo_g=&emci=a339868d-8b93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=984652cc-8d93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) and the state has a [D- rated Yes, please!**

**Opportunities with INDems**
### **Regional Organizing Directors**
**The Indiana Democratic Party is hiring Regional Organizing Directors to to build a stronger party in the field throughout the Hoosier State. The role is ideal for someone looking to be more involved in politics and party building and who wants to dedicate themselves to learning more about the inner workings of campaigns, strategic planning, and voter targeting and contact. These are important roles to building back a fair and equitable two party system of government in Indiana. ** **For full job descriptions and how to apply visit our website at[https://www.indems.org/jobs/](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57301406/387478669/1076440731?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ODQ2NTJjYy04ZDkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hi3DBJB3TNK_GqUYvm6FuUbSpPXrgehVFuHYOWsRo_g=&emci=a339868d-8b93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=984652cc-8d93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)**
### **Executive Director**
**The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Executive Director (ED) to lead our team. In partnership with the Chair and State Central Committee, the ED is responsible for deploying the strategy, overall management, and growth of IDP. The ED will be involved in all aspects of the Party’s operations, including but not limited to: election activities, voter protection, fundraising, budgeting, staff recruitment and retention, outreach, and building party infrastructure. ** **General Duties: **
* **Responsible for overall operational efficiency of IDP, including staff and creation and execution of Party strategy.**
* **Recruits, interviews, hires, and manages IDP and coordinated campaign staff. **
* **Manages day-to-day work of Communications, Data & Organizing, Operations, and Finance teams.**
* **Oversees the day-to-day work of outside consultants and vendors. **
* **Serves as one of the spokespeople for IDP to the media. **
* **Other responsibilities as required. **

**Party Affairs**
* **Coordinates work and materials for the Democratic State Central Committee, District leaders, County leaders, and precinct committeemen and women. **
* **Keeps up strong relationships with various constituencies and stakeholders around the state, including organized labor, African-American leaders, Latino leaders, LGBTQ Hoosiers, women’s groups, Indiana Young Democrats, and College Democrats.**
* **Maintains close working relationships with various party partner organizations including, but not limited to: DNC, ASDC, DCCC, DSCC, DGA, and DLCC.**
* **Builds and nurtures relationships with Democratic candidates, elected officials, Democratic caucuses, and staff members across the state.**
* **Supports the building, growth, and success of county parties throughout the state. **
* **Oversees an annual party retreat, annual fundraising dinner, biennial state conventions, and the National Delegate Selection process for the 2024 Democratic convention. **

**Campaign & Election Activities**
* **Shares regular organizing, training, coaching, and other resources online and in-person for Party staff, volunteers, and party leadership.**
* **Works with IDP and Indiana Election Division to ensure the Party is up-to-date on the current and latest rules relating to elections and voting in Indiana.**
* **Troubleshoots any issues with campaigns or county parties on behalf of IDP to ensure the Party is represented well and that issues are resolved in a timely fashion. **
* **Oversees voter protection in-state and in partnership with DNC guidance.**
* **Oversees GOTV and Election Day efforts and events for IDP. **

**Fundraising**
* **Works with the State Chair to raise money for the party through call time, meetings, events, memberships, online giving, and other income generating streams. **
* **Partners with the finance team to create a robust apparatus for fundraising and cultivating large and small donors.**
* **Represents IDP at party, media, or community functions across the state. **

**Operations & Compliance**
* **Manages office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces.**
* **Keeps an updated and working knowledge of campaign finance, election, and compliance laws and works in concert with the compliance team and legal counsel to ensure all Party activities are consistently compliant. **
* **Works with the State Chair and Party CPA and Treasurer to implement the budget. **

**Qualifications:** **Minimum of at least four (4) years with direct campaign experience. Executive experience working on a coordinated campaign, statewide or Congressional political campaign, or with a state political party preferred. **
* **Experience managing a growing, diverse, and highly skilled team, with demonstrated ability to achieve high levels of performance and outcomes through guidance and management. **
* **A reputation for leading with integrity and making decisions with the input of the appropriate stakeholders. **
* **Experience building, managing, and tracking large budgets. **
* **Excellent verbal, written, and analytical skills. **
* **Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time. **
* **Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment. **
* **Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks. **
* **Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism. **
* **Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality. **

**Salary:** **Salary is generally competitive and commensurate with experience. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. ** **Application Instructions: ** **Please email your resume and three professional references to[jobs@indems.org](mailto:jobs@indems.org) with “Executive Director” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP.**

body:
it’s just a matter of time...
| Folks, here's what you need to know:

**ONE:** Ron DeSantis is one of the cruelest MAGA leaders of our lifetime.

body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57301611/387479500/553882890?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDQxOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMjU0NzViNC04ZjkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uadqx_4WJb47QnLMhJU5e7kJESZXgS4_kZdhAOAgnCM=&emci=8c2799b7-8a93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a25475b4-8f93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=9636579)

| | Peter, It was a tremendous honor to give my victory speech on election night in Springfield with Congressman Peter DeFazio at my side. Peter has been a principled, independent leader for our district. He served with distinction in Congress for the last 36 years, and as a Lane County Commissioner before that. He took on presidents (of both parties), corporate greed, Wall Street billionaires, and MAGA extremists. His steadfast advocacy made him some powerful enemies and he was the first Congressman to be attacked by a superPAC. It didn't faze him. Nothing did. He set a high bar for what our district expects from our representative in Congress. **That's why I'm so humbled by the chance to build on the work that Peter has done.** When I decided to run for Congress, I vowed to continue the same style of leadership that has been Peter's hallmark. No nonsense, no grandstanding. Like Peter, I've always been focused on getting the job done, not on who gets the credit._And we've got a lot of work to do._ Peter, as a leader, my number one job is to be transparent. You can trust that when things are going well, I'll gladly share the good news. And when things aren't going well, you'll hear that from me as well, I'll be honest with you about the threats we face. Just last week, Congress was held hostage by the most extreme wing of the Republican caucus. Our country froze to a standstill. **While the Republicans in Congress eventually got their act together (for now), this remains a perilous time for our nation.** We need to be honest about the threat that MAGA Republicans continue to pose to our democracy and our fundamental freedoms. We need to be honest about the urgency of the climate crisis, as we see the impacts right in front of us every day. We need to protect programs like Social Security and Medicare that Americans have paid into their whole lives. And we have to be honest that our economy works very well for the people at the top, at the expense of the rest of us. **That's why when it comes to tackling the largest challenges we're facing, you can trust that I'll be transparent, hardworking and never forget where I come from.** **After all, I learned from the best.** _Val_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, name: OcasioCortez.com
subject: More progressives in Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
This doesn’t happen on its own.
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20230113-3795-header&t=1&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)
There’s nothing better than when a bunch of new awesome people join your friend group.
That’s what happened last week as Alexandria and her colleagues welcomed an exciting class of newly elected progressive members to Congress.
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230113.jF4ibV&refcode=em20230113-3795-P_FND_SPL_CTC&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)
From Delia Ramirez (IL-03) to Summer Lee (PA-12) to Greg Casar (TX-35), and beyond — we are thrilled to grow our movement of leaders in Congress who don’t accept money from corporate PACs, will fight for working-people, and fight for transformative change.
But this doesn’t happen on its own — and we’re still a long way from having the numbers we need to pass policies like Medicare for All and a Green New Deal.
[That’s why we need your help to recruit and support more progressive candidates so we can grow our “friend group” even bigger. Can you split a contribution to Courage to Change and our campaign today?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230113.jF4ibV&refcode=em20230113-3795-P_FND_SPL_CTC&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230113.jF4ibV&refcode=em20230113-3795-P_FND_SPL_CTC&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)**
Alexandria experienced firsthand how difficult it is for working-class, grassroots candidates to break into politics and compete in a corrupt system where billionaires can buy elections.
So she decided to launch Courage to Change in January 2020: **a leadership PAC that supports candidates who refuse to bow to establishment pressure, who advocate ferociously for working-class families, and who have lived the same struggles as the people they seek to represent.**
Here’s a little bit more about some of the Courage to Change candidates who joined us in Congress this year:
– **Summer Lee (PA-12):** Summer is a former Pennsylvania state legislator born and raised in the Mon Valley, in the heart of Pennsylvania’s 12th District. She is a dedicated organizer and attorney and the first Black woman to represent Pennsylvania in Congress.
– **Greg Casar (TX-35):** Greg is a native Texan, son of Mexican immigrants, and community organizer. He served on the Austin City Council for seven years and is committed to bringing much-needed change to Texas.
– **Delia Ramirez (IL-03):** Delia is an accomplished social service director and daughter of Guatemalan immigrant parents who has dedicated her life and career to advocating for working families. She served in the Illinois State Legislature for three years where she won Medicaid coverage for undocumented seniors, and hundreds of millions of dollars in rental and mortgage relief for people across Illinois.
**We need to lay the groundwork now so we’re ready for 2024.** [Can you please split a $3 contribution between Courage to Change and our campaign right now to help us elect progressive community leaders, activists, and working-class candidates across the country?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230113.jF4ibV&refcode=em20230113-3795-P_FND_SPL_CTC&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)
Thank you so much for your support.
– Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230113?refcode=em20230113-3795-P_FND_SPL_CTC&t=24&refcode2=3795_2526796_8Fxtps&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230113-3795-P_FND_SPL_CTC&t=25&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=26&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=27&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=28&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=29&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=30&akid=3795%2E2526796%2E8Fxtps)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress

She served in the Illinois State Legislature for three years where she won Medicaid coverage for undocumented seniors, and hundreds of millions of dollars in rental and mortgage relief for people across Illinois. We need to lay the groundwork now so we’re ready for 2024. Thank you so much for your support. – Team AOC | ---|--- | | ---|---|--- Email us: mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com

name: Hakeem Jeffries
subject: RE: humbly asking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/38348?t=6&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2) Saturday, I was sworn in as the new Democratic Leader.

Today, I promise you this: House Democrats will always fight for you and your family.

**But right now, I’m humbly asking you to fight with us.** [**After Republicans’ historically dysfunctional start, everyone will be watching to see what kind of fundraising numbers Democrats post. The support you show in my first 168 hours as Leader is essential to reclaiming the House for Democrats so we can continue to deliver For The People. I’m asking: Will you split $10 between myself and Voter Protection Project make this a historic fundraising period for Democrats? »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/38348?t=7&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2) Here’s the deal:

Speaker McCarthy is rushing to outraise us to prove that we don’t have what it takes to reclaim the Majority.

**_We’re going to prove him absolutely wrong._**

Which is why we need a powerful surge of support – 1,000 gifts in my first crucial days as Leader – to kick this year off with a strong foundation for victory, outraise extreme MAGA Republicans and hand them their first upset of the year.

Can I count on you to step up in this defining moment? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Hakeem Jeffries and Voter Protection Project will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/38349?t=8&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2)

Keep the faith,
Hakeem

(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=13&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=6014%2E878179%2Ea23Pg2)

body:
TIMtalk will keep you updated on the important issues and goings-on in our campaign, in Tennessee, and in our country.| | | [](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=c54d43a727be4757bf6aaf9f82c2c754&_e=UmLBk8rvjBQaELDdgtWMDW00xtyeSEsPSJFsrtVnuvF-YaNCDgJEqDtmmxb4tE1JbgoTTRfW_78uv0m1DoJj7bJZn-WlTAAdR1M39RRsNjXcnBhJEzsONYEEVgEKNOJj7BuZ4jIhysxO9QHrsoId_MgLpThlo2TffItqoPUaqXtsbSwCsAZhZqHnGCJgDVUm6JfnwaHbExL981C2U00t5ofE226NYcgouvVRwBjLw8itJN2khCFABxSbptwmtaZi9_k_IqZyBWOOmJDoMjO3s9aua2lrSQ1_s5WpCdtsSraLoU6Y8T3UxoGbHk1UD9OPsY1I_fQcsr3bKoRSOvYWdg%3D%3D)

| | **Biden’s Hand Caught In The Cookie Jar 🤬**
---
| |
---
Democrats joined Republicans in the House this week to pass a bill that would prevent the Biden administration from selling any more oil from our Strategic Petroleum Reserve to [China or Chinese-owned companies](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=c54d43a727be4757bf6aaf9f82c2c754&_e=UmLBk8rvjBQaELDdgtWMDaG_ZfFZ2jul3X4Mp9z4asZK_UocoV_28GwfeX7M8E6aTEQc_-xG3X-LIvFxbZA8nqEVynal1b-yz6vqGJvrVPLjTk-0SlU96yT3ksYAgfjAr5u-v9e5NFqWv-awoWiym33fuGzJ3FyvBh_nFoDggKbnG1YVH5x59j3akDi1vE8TT_co-vMb6QrBZp3NdtSVB6lHjA9UfsXFXavzs58aUEVa3Axxp08QVBJaZmB8bPdn4eXqcV3K_Lg4ad3oU3UwgA%3D%3D). Hallelujah! It baffles me that the Biden administration (_already selling nearly 200 million barrels in 2022_) wants to dip back in for seconds to shell out [over 40%](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=c54d43a727be4757bf6aaf9f82c2c754&_e=UmLBk8rvjBQaELDdgtWMDaG_ZfFZ2jul3X4Mp9z4asa265q4QM1NpwQEuZQHoDBgn6BQ4qCvmnnWFgXBm8TRGem4-Ltfat9DI3a0BPEP3KSB1Pu58pB27miYFaSGRqKsrfirwcUCdWGco9rSCTFsKTo6Ni1HsgdhqiUQKPK_tflUFFfnNgmnyxgfre-ZWuOyeFLbn7U7HNgC-0nqQVx0JyIW3vwp8PejYn3ehVL1o_a6rpopLZlvVAE4l7RFlDVuxv137b8fpCtqjQZp3Ae0CTBWfbqcxXeQz00Grk6RmI1QuTPgkDHzrya-jzu6vaYH) of our oil reserves to the communist regime. Selling our oil, which is now at its [lowest level](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=c54d43a727be4757bf6aaf9f82c2c754&_e=UmLBk8rvjBQaELDdgtWMDWxb2Omn0pLciN7zrvqhy1lWG3PVrqsiqSKisVlRJUH_szFAmwWQ4IVq-f90AOuouOv9aoMZTM3t2rxczM845vG8suJ8oAYnlBotPO3XaG83-2xMn4Rau3zbIrNTN8ct11tUGfVJDkJaclGNmuJNlm9q-9jJeF5NBu-nsaUxftSspK7rtqTX4HEny71KR_oNJ97GrO_wN77b0m2vJ1lsGhkFFPvtU0z8rtUH9D3WxGoddBpIVPRnwTIIUPxPipedKjgyN0CSxs8CMnik1riLrc09YTAl6kUgPdCUT8XooEURmTXRuyYAf8ObRCV9CUxPZA%3D%3D) since the 1980s, puts our national security at risk and empowers China. Why on God's green earth would anyone agree to that? name: Sherrod Brown
subject: The last remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH |
body:
Ohio is already shaping up to be a must-win battleground.
| | |
---
I’m up for re-election in 2024, friend, and as one of just three Democrats in states Trump won and the only remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio, I’m a prime target for Mitch McConnell and the GOP. (_There’s already a wealthy Republican state senator considering running against me._)

NBC says my race could be “even nastier and more expensive” than this year’s Senate race in Ohio – and that race was one of the most expensive in the country with over $200 million spent.

**[Frankly, it’s going to be my toughest race yet, but if we work together, we can win in Ohio. Will you split a contribution between my campaign and Maggie Hassan's as we begin the new year?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57302749/387483389/-1928913949?amounts=3,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2184-bu-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NGI5N2MwYS05NjkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aQkQ8dHmpEVEhgCf1BM1v3rUP4_S14MVAHyaHlUwuVA=)**

I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio. I went to school with the sons and daughters of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. Eventually, those jobs disappeared as one big plant after another left town. Every day, I fight in the U.S. Senate to bring those jobs back to our state and to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families.

I wear a canary pin that a steelworker gave to me years ago – a reminder that workers used to have to take a canary with them to the coal mines in case there was dangerous gas. The pin is a symbol of the progress we’ve made on workers’ rights and the work we still have left to do.

Like raising wages. Making sure everyone has good job opportunities in their hometowns. Rewarding companies that invest in American jobs instead of shipping them overseas. Ensuring no one needs a second or third job just to get by.

That’s what the Dignity of Work is all about, friend. No matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do – whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips – your work has dignity and I’ll fight for you.

**[I need you in this fight, too, friend. It won’t be easy, but if we organize, work hard, and start early, I know we can do this. Please consider chipping in our average online contribution of $26 – or whatever amount makes the most sense for you.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57302750/387483390/-1928913949?amounts=3,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2184-bu-nd&recurring=0&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NGI5N2MwYS05NjkzLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aQkQ8dHmpEVEhgCf1BM1v3rUP4_S14MVAHyaHlUwuVA=)**

clean_body: Ohio is already shaping up to be a must-win battleground. | | | I’m up for re-election in 2024, friend, and as one of just three Democrats in states Trump won and the only remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio, I’m a prime target for Mitch McConnell and the GOP. (There’s already a wealthy Republican state senator considering running against me.) NBC says my race could be “even nastier and more expensive” than this year’s Senate race in Ohio – and that race was one of the most expensive in the country with over $200 million spent. = I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio. I went to school with the sons and daughters of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. Eventually, those jobs disappeared as one big plant after another left town. Every day, I fight in the U.S. Senate to bring those jobs back to our state and to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families. I wear a canary pin that a steelworker gave to me years ago – a reminder that workers used to have to take a canary with them to the coal mines in case there was dangerous gas. The pin is a symbol of the progress we’ve made on workers’ rights and the work we still have left to do. Like raising wages. Making sure everyone has good job opportunities in their hometowns. Rewarding companies that invest in American jobs instead of shipping them overseas. Ensuring no one needs a second or third job just to get by. That’s what the Dignity of Work is all about, friend. No matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do – whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips – your work has dignity and I’ll fight for you. = With gratitude, Sherrod | If you'd like to support Maggie Hassan for Senate, =. = If you’re reading this from your phone, to automatically sign up for our SMS list. name: Team Sherrod
subject: Official Team Sherrod Newsletter:
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87645/images/the-canary-chronicle-FIN.png]
The Official Campaign Newsletter of Senator Sherrod Brown
Headlines from Ohio & Across the Country
MSNBC: “'It's what bipartisanship looks like': Senator on new bridge project” [https://www.msnbc.com/morning-joe/watch/biden-mcconnell-highlight-bipartisanship-at-kentucky-event-159323717744]
Sherrod appeared on MSNBC to discuss the progress being made on Ohio’s new Brent Spence Bridge, a project that will improve commutes and transportation for Ohioans and local businesses. It was made possible by the bipartisan infrastructure bill signed into law last year and Sherrod’s provision to rebuild and upgrade thousands of deteriorating bridges in Ohio and across the country.
WATCH NOW [https://www.msnbc.com/morning-joe/watch/biden-mcconnell-highlight-bipartisanship-at-kentucky-event-159323717744]
https://www.msnbc.com/morning-joe/watch/biden-mcconnell-highlight-bipartisanship-at-kentucky-event-159323717744 [https://www.msnbc.com/morning-joe/watch/biden-mcconnell-highlight-bipartisanship-at-kentucky-event-159323717744]
Ohio Capital Journal: “Biden signs law extending marriage protections” [https://ohiocapitaljournal.com/2022/12/14/biden-signs-law-extending-marriage-protections-to-same-sex-and-interracial-couples/]
Everyone should be able to marry whom they love. Sherrod knows that -- that’s why he’s been a champion for marriage equality for years, and recently, he helped pass and send the Respect for Marriage Act to President Biden’s desk to protect same-sex and interracial marriages in the face of the Supreme Court’s threats to marriage equality.
Spectrum News 1: “Brown renews push for expanded child tax credit” [https://spectrumnews1.com/oh/columbus/news/2022/12/07/ohio-sen--brown-renews-push-expanded-child-tax-credit]
When Congress expanded the Child Tax Credit, it cut child poverty nearly in half, and over 90% of Ohio families with children saw more money in their pockets for things like groceries and child care. Sherrod is continuing his efforts to extend the expanded CTC to give Ohio families a little more breathing room.
Fulton County Expositor : “Connecting Ohio veterans with PACT Act benefits” [https://www.fcnews.org/opinion/columns/40513/connecting-ohio-veterans-with-pact-act-benefits]
Last year, Sherrod led the passage of the PACT Act, the most comprehensive expansion of benefits for veterans exposed to toxic pollution in U.S. history. Read Sherrod’s op-ed on what this new law will mean for vets in Ohio and across the country.
State of the Race(s) in Ohio
OH-SEN UPDATE: According to NBC, next year’s Ohio Senate race is expected to be “even nastier and more expensive” than last year’s contest, and the Hill named Sherrod one of the “most vulnerable” Senate Democrats up for re-election next year. What does it all mean? We need to start raising the resources to run a strong, winning campaign -- now.
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbe230113nl?refcode=sbe230113nl]
Get Involved
Have you taken our Official Supporter Survey yet? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/QSHORPlKU0-tHHT_pg8N8g2] We’re asking members of this community about the issues at the top of their priority lists in 2023. We’ve heard responses like climate change, dark money, and gun violence. Now, we want to hear yours.
TAKE THE SURVEY [https://secure.ngpvan.com/QSHORPlKU0-tHHT_pg8N8g2]
We’re hitting the ground running and building Sherrod’s campaign from the ground up -- including Sherrod’s Social Team, which will play a key role in amplifying our message and expanding our reach over the next two years. If you haven’t already, sign up today! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/bUzStTJtW0aYD8cCkdkJ-g2?emci=119b10e9-397a-ed11-819c-000d3a9eb474&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=%7B%7BContactsEmailID%7D%7D]
JOIN SHERROD’S SOCIAL TEAM [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbe230113nl?refcode=sbe230113nl]
Enjoying the Canary Chronicle?
Share this online and help spread the word!
https://www.facebook.com/sherrod [https://www.facebook.com/sherrod] https://twitter.com/SherrodBrown [https://twitter.com/SherrodBrown] https://www.instagram.com/sherrod [https://www.instagram.com/sherrod]
You’re receiving these updates because you’re a dedicated member of Team Sherrod. Please take a moment to add info@sherrodbrown.com [info@sherrodbrown.com] to your contact list to make sure you don’t miss any updates from the team.

Friends of Sherrod Brown
3867 West Market Street
Ste. 289
Akron, OH 44333
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please
unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/tClQUZwGoEOS_rmuR1aPwA2?unsubscribedata=zkGuJsRddKPGOmm2Uz8G2FYc7%2FDj2JIhdKKe8bNIML2i7MtuirqtrdioLzymzxJu9Br0BEmOruzsE5No06XtXttzl8Nqjv8FS%2Fcz3XwVkiFX%2BbILCctguCXfVsAd1eaZdbTfYCuBCOBfwgsOTin06%2FMoU2PtuH8epCM9%2B2BXPZo7J%2BgGgQJYoj29ee6HHubuebv9ESbBsP%2F037lOfiuCMFWautZnPgyrbrBx6ERkL6Ag5bTt%2BDQRQTMBKD39CUobGqfOnqJAcFVqwp8mpfOKi0dHU2r%2BNkm6E8Nt3LqC2I9wOn0avac2vozZI94W3R%2BdbF%2B%2BKxBEAOz3YGaQje69XZgHpwGrD6gFYtHf10gvTzcJKkk7om%2FTSbraMSOUCsp%2BG369ulrA9FDv4dt82whrNrgr%2FxjUzKanAegJjjDqQiaoFtvGn5QfogeqUomBO6Tp .

name: Geoff Simpson
subject: A steady decline
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Hi, Peter! It’s
Geoff again.

Just a few weeks ago we launched our 2024 Candidate Recruitment. We’ve
been receiving nominations from across the country and are doing intensive
data analysis to determine which districts to target and where to focus
our resources.

But here’s the problem we’re having: with our fundraising declining over
the last year, we’re in a bit of a tough spot.

Look, we don’t take a cent from corporate PACs and we don’t have
billionaire backers who can make up the difference when our fundraising
goes down. We truly rely on contributions from people like you, donating
whatever you can afford.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/124786?refcode=em20230113-27043&t=1001&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B ]Do you want more fierce progressives in Congress, fighting for your
interests alongside the Squad? Please, donate $3 right now.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/124786?refcode=em20230113-27043&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B ]Contribute $3

As the director for our campaigns department, I need to make sure we have
the resources to recruit more candidates like Jamaal Bowman, Cori Bush,
Summer Lee, and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez — who were all recruited by JD.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/124786?refcode=em20230113-27043&t=1021&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B ]Will you donate $3 right now to help us raise $8,000
over email by midnight on the 15th? This is a steep goal but if everyone
on this team steps up, we can get there together.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/124786?refcode=em20230113-27043&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1040&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B ]Contribute $3

I wouldn’t be reaching out if this goal wasn’t really
important,
Peter. Thank you for helping
out.

More soon,

Geoff Simpson

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1041&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1042&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230113-27043-footer&t=1043&refcode2=27043_1203867_VolE5B&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1044&akid=27043%2E1203867%2EVolE5B

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com
